mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon; the signal is always active.
failure_condition: If RSAFS rises month-over-month but the S&P 500 20-day return is negative in more than 55% of such instances, or if the rank IC over any 3-year rolling window is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds 6.5%, it signals a severely weak labor market that depresses consumer spending and earnings, pushing S&P 500 lower over the following quarter; the signal activates only above the threshold.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE is above 6.5%, the S&P 500 60-day return is positive more than 60% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of a very low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL) creates negative real rates, which signals impending monetary tightening and represses equity valuations; the interaction is always evaluated.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month inflation is above 0.3%, the S&P 500 20-day return is positive in more than half of the cases, or the interaction term in a linear regression is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 2%), rising housing starts (HOUST) signal residential investment strength that boosts the economy and lifts S&P 500 returns over a quarter; the regime condition must hold for activation.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 2%, a positive month-over-month change in HOUST is followed by a negative 60-day S&P 500 return more than 40% of the time, or the signal's win rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing activity, which leads to negative corporate earnings surprises and depresses S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon; the signal triggers only on negative changes.
failure_condition: After a decline in INDPRO, the S&P 500 5-day return is positive more than half the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer income and spending, pushing S&P 500 returns higher over a 20-day horizon; the signal is always on.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS increases month-over-month but the S&P 500 20-day return is negative in more than 55% of instances, or the rank IC over any 2-year rolling window is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising retail sales (RSAFS) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) jointly signals a robust economic expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days; the interaction is always considered.
failure_condition: In periods when RSAFS rises and UNRATE declines simultaneously, the S&P 500 20-day return is negative more than 40% of the time, or the interaction coefficient in a regression is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.5% (near the zero lower bound), a rising real GDP (GDPC1) signals that the economy is recovering from a downturn, which lifts S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon; the regime condition must hold.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, a quarterly increase in GDPC1 is followed by a negative 60-day S&P 500 return more than 30% of the time, or the average return in such periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).
